{"ok": true, "tool_used": "regex", "suspicious_summary": "# Pre-filtered suspicious candidates (external/public + state-changing + no obvious access-control modifier):
- `initialize` (visibility=external, modifiers=initializer)
- `pledgeAndBorrow` (visibility=external, modifiers=nonReentrant)
- `auction` (visibility=external, modifiers=nonReentrant, whenNotPaused)
- `withdrawNFT` (visibility=external, modifiers=nonReentrant, whenNotPaused)
- `getOrderDetail` (visibility=external, modifiers=address, collection, uint256, tokenId, address, pledger)
- `notifyOrderLiquidated` (visibility=external, modifiers=none)
- `notifyRepayBorrow` (visibility=external, modifiers=none)
- `isOrderLiquidated` (visibility=public, modifiers=bool)
- `getOrderState` (visibility=external, modifiers=OrderState, orderState)
- `airDrop` (visibility=public, modifiers=none)

The ground-truth vulnerable function is highly likely to be in the list above. Use this as your primary candidate pool when ranking top-3."}

tool_args.hypothesis: If an attacker can become controller (via an unprotected setter), they can call notifyOrderLiquidated to mark orders liquidated and transfer pledged NFTs to themselves or to arbitrary auctionWinner, enabling theft of pledged NFTs.

steps.tool_result: {"ok": true, "target_function": "notifyOrderLiquidated", "hypothesis": "If an attacker can become controller (via an unprotected setter), they can call notifyOrderLiquidated to mark orders liquidated and transfer pledged NFTs to themselves or to arbitrary auctionWinner, e"}

tool_args.exploit_logic: 1) Deploy XNFT and a fake controller. 2) Create an order by calling pledge (mocking the NFT transfer by using a simple ERC721 test token). 3) Make the fake controller call notifyOrderLiquidated(orderId, liquidatedPrice, liquidator, xToken) on XNFT, since notifyOrderLiquidated only checks msg.sender == controller. 4) Verify that the LiquidatedOrder is set and auctionWinner/transfer occurred if liquidator == pledger or stolen to liquidator depending on logic. The PoC will set liquidator to attacker and assert that the pledged token was transferred to attacker.

tool_args.reason: Cannot compile original contract for PoC: forge test fails due to missing imported files (src/interface/* and library files) referenced by XNFT; without those sources I cannot build a test against the original. Cannot prove exploit within budget.
